subjects.name: Mathematics
subjects.description: Pure mathematics, mechanics and statistics across three linear papers.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Mathematics (7357) covers pure maths (two-thirds), statistics (one-sixth) and mechanics (one-sixth). Topics span proof, algebra, coordinate geometry, sequences, trigonometry, exponentials, differentiation, integration, numerical methods, vectors, statistical sampling, distributions, hypothesis testing, kinematics, forces and moments. The course is linear with three 2-hour papers at the end of Year 13.

topics.title: Algebra & Functions
topics.summary: Indices, surds, quadratics, partial fractions, polynomial division, modulus, transformations and composite functions.

topics.title: Differentiation
topics.summary: Rates of change, chain/product/quotient rules, implicit differentiation, parametric equations and stationary points.

topics.title: Integration
topics.summary: Definite and indefinite integrals, areas under curves, integration by substitution and parts, solving differential equations.

topics.title: Trigonometry
topics.summary: Identities, radian measure, compound and double-angle formulae, solving trigonometric equations and modelling.

topics.title: Mechanics
topics.summary: Kinematics in one and two dimensions, forces, Newton's laws, moments, projectiles and variable acceleration.

topics.title: Statistics
topics.summary: Probability distributions, hypothesis testing, correlation, regression, the normal distribution and data interpretation.

resources.name: Physics and Maths Tutor - A-Level Maths Notes

subjects.name: Physics
subjects.description: Mechanics, fields, thermodynamics, waves and modern physics.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Physics (7408) is a context-free specification offering practical core content and optional topics such as Astrophysics or Medical Physics. It covers measurements, particles, waves, mechanics, electricity, thermal physics, fields, nuclear physics and an optional topic. Assessment is through three 2-hour papers plus practical endorsement.

topics.title: Mechanics
topics.summary: Motion, momentum, energy, equilibrium, Newton's laws, circular motion and simple harmonic motion.

topics.title: Electricity
topics.summary: Current, potential difference, resistance, circuits, EMF, internal resistance and the behaviour of semiconductors.

topics.title: Waves
topics.summary: Superposition, interference, diffraction, standing waves, the photoelectric effect and quantum behaviour of light.

topics.title: Fields
topics.summary: Gravitational, electric and magnetic fields, field strength, potential, orbital mechanics and electromagnetic induction.

topics.title: Nuclear Physics
topics.summary: Radioactivity, decay equations, half-life, mass-energy equivalence, fission, fusion and safety considerations.

topics.title: Thermal Physics
topics.summary: Internal energy, ideal gas laws, specific heat capacity, latent heat and the kinetic theory of gases.

resources.name: Physics and Maths Tutor - A-Level Physics

subjects.name: Chemistry
subjects.description: Physical, inorganic and organic chemistry with practical skills.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Chemistry (7405) is divided into physical, inorganic and organic chemistry. Students study atomic structure, bonding, energetics, kinetics, equilibria, redox, thermodynamics, transition metals, organic synthesis, polymers, proteins, NMR and chromatography. Practical skills are assessed within written papers and through a separate practical endorsement.

topics.title: Atomic Structure
topics.summary: Electron configuration, ionisation energy, mass spectrometry, time-of-flight calculations and quantum behaviour.

topics.title: Bonding
topics.summary: Ionic, covalent and metallic bonding, shapes of molecules, intermolecular forces and evidence from physical properties.

topics.title: Energetics
topics.summary: Enthalpy changes, Hess's law, Born-Haber cycles, bond enthalpies, calorimetry and enthalpy of solution.

topics.title: Kinetics & Equilibria
topics.summary: Rates of reaction, collision theory, rate equations, Le Chatelier's principle, Kc, Kp and equilibrium constants.

topics.title: Organic Chemistry
topics.summary: Functional groups, reaction mechanisms, isomerism, stereochemistry, optical isomerism and synthesis pathways.

topics.title: Analysis
topics.summary: NMR spectroscopy, infrared spectroscopy, mass spectrometry, chromatography and identifying unknown compounds.

resources.name: Physics and Maths Tutor - Chemistry Notes

subjects.name: Biology
subjects.description: Cells, organisms, genetics, ecology and homeostasis.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Biology (7402) covers biological molecules, cells, transport, genetics, energy transfers, homeostasis, populations, evolution, ecosystems and gene expression. It develops practical competency through 12 required practicals and assesses data analysis, essay writing and synoptic understanding across three papers.

topics.title: Biological Molecules
topics.summary: Carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, enzymes, DNA structure, RNA and the importance of water and inorganic ions.

topics.title: Cells
topics.summary: Ultrastructure, transport across membranes, cell division, the cell cycle, microscopy and investigating cell organisation.

topics.title: Genetics
topics.summary: DNA replication, protein synthesis, inheritance patterns, mutations, genetic diversity and gene expression control.

topics.title: Exchange & Transport
topics.summary: Gas exchange, circulation, mass transport in plants and animals, surface-area-to-volume ratio and adaptation.

topics.title: Ecology
topics.summary: Ecosystems, populations, succession, nutrient cycling, energy transfer, biodiversity and conservation strategies.

topics.title: Homeostasis
topics.summary: Nervous and hormonal control, feedback mechanisms, blood glucose regulation, osmoregulation and the kidney.

resources.name: Physics and Maths Tutor - Biology Notes

subjects.name: English Literature
subjects.description: Prose, poetry, drama and critical theory across genres and periods.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level English Literature A (7712) explores literary genres such as tragedy and crime writing, alongside love through the ages. Students study Shakespeare, pre-1900 and post-2000 prose and poetry, unseen texts, and complete a non-exam assessment comparing two texts informed by critical theory. The specification builds analytical, evaluative and synoptic essay skills.

topics.title: Poetry Analysis
topics.summary: Form, meter, imagery, symbolism, comparative reading and the relationship between poet and reader.

topics.title: Prose
topics.summary: Narrative voice, structure, thematic development, realism, stream of consciousness and social context in novels.

topics.title: Drama
topics.summary: Dramatic method, character construction, stagecraft, audience response and the role of the tragic hero.

topics.title: Shakespeare
topics.summary: Close reading of a set play, critical interpretations, performance history and connections to dramatic genre.

topics.title: Critical Theory
topics.summary: Applying feminist, Marxist, post-colonial, structuralist and eco-critical lenses to literary texts.

topics.title: Contexts
topics.summary: Historical, social, political and literary contexts, including how texts are shaped by and respond to their era.

resources.name: AQA A-Level English Literature A Specification (7712)
AQA English Literature A Critical Anthology

subjects.name: History
subjects.description: Modern and early modern British, European and world history.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level History (7042) offers breadth and depth through component groupings such as Tudor England, the Industrial Revolution, world wars and the Cold War. Students develop skills in evaluating source reliability, provenance and utility, constructing arguments using historiography, and writing extended essays. A coursework component allows independent research on a topic of the student's choice.

topics.title: Sources & Evidence
topics.summary: Evaluating reliability, provenance, utility and value of primary and secondary historical sources.

topics.title: Tudor England
topics.summary: Religious change, government and rebellion under the Tudor monarchs, 1485–1603.

topics.title: Industrial Revolution
topics.summary: Economic and social change in 18th–19th century Britain, urbanisation and the birth of modern industry.

topics.title: World Wars
topics.summary: Causes, course and consequences of the First and Second World Wars, 1914–1945.

topics.title: Cold War
topics.summary: Superpower tension, crises, détente, the arms race and the eventual collapse of the Soviet bloc.

topics.title: Coursework Skills
topics.summary: Constructing independent arguments, using historiography and evaluating interpretations over time.

subjects.name: Geography
subjects.description: Physical and human geography with fieldwork and independent investigation.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Geography (7037) covers physical systems including water and carbon cycles, coastal landscapes and hazards, and human systems including globalisation, urban environments and changing places. Students complete an independent fieldwork investigation and develop quantitative, qualitative and cartographic skills. The specification is synoptic, linking topics across physical and human geography.

topics.title: Water & Carbon Cycles
topics.summary: Systems, stores, flows, human impacts, climate change feedbacks and the role of tropical rainforests.

topics.title: Coastal Landscapes
topics.summary: Erosion, deposition, landforms, sea-level change, management strategies and conflict over coastlines.

topics.title: Hazards
topics.summary: Tectonic and atmospheric hazards, responses, vulnerability, risk management and disaster resilience.

topics.title: Globalisation
topics.summary: Trade, TNCs, global interdependence, international migration and the impacts of global governance.

topics.title: Urban Environments
topics.summary: Urbanisation, regeneration, sustainability, smart cities and the challenges of megacities.

topics.title: Fieldwork
topics.summary: Data collection, presentation, statistical analysis, independent investigation and risk assessment.

subjects.name: Economics
subjects.description: Microeconomics, macroeconomics and international economics with real-world application.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Economics (7136) combines microeconomics (individual and firm behaviour, market structures, labour markets, market failure) with macroeconomics (national income, aggregate demand and supply, inflation, unemployment, financial markets, international trade and development). Students apply quantitative skills and evaluate government policy using contemporary case studies.

topics.title: Supply & Demand
topics.summary: Price mechanism, elasticity, consumer and producer surplus, market equilibrium and government intervention.

topics.title: Market Failure
topics.summary: Externalities, public goods, merit and demerit goods, information asymmetry and government failure.

topics.title: National Income
topics.summary: GDP, the circular flow, aggregate demand and supply, Keynesian and classical models, and the multiplier.

topics.title: Inflation & Unemployment
topics.summary: Causes, measurement, Phillips curve trade-offs, natural rate and hysteresis.

topics.title: International Trade
topics.summary: Comparative advantage, exchange rates, balance of payments, protectionism and trading blocs.

topics.title: Fiscal & Monetary Policy
topics.summary: Government spending, taxation, interest rates, quantitative easing and supply-side policies.

subjects.name: Computer Science
subjects.description: Programming, theory, systems, networks, databases and a practical project.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Computer Science (7517) develops computational thinking through programming in Python, VB.NET, C# or Java. Students study data structures, algorithms, theory of computation, data representation, computer systems, networking, databases, big data and functional programming. A non-exam assessment (20%) requires students to solve a real problem through a systematic development project.

topics.title: Programming
topics.summary: Data types, control structures, functions, object-oriented programming, recursion and exception handling.

topics.title: Data Structures
topics.summary: Arrays, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, hash tables, dictionaries, vectors and dynamic versus static structures.

topics.title: Algorithms
topics.summary: Searching, sorting, pathfinding, Big-O complexity, optimisation and standard algorithms including Dijkstra and A*.

topics.title: Computer Systems
topics.summary: CPU architecture, memory, the fetch-execute cycle, machine code, assembly language and logic gates.

topics.title: Networks
topics.summary: Protocols, TCP/IP stack, layers, packet switching, security, encryption and the Internet of Things.

topics.title: Databases
topics.summary: Relational models, normalisation, SQL, entity-relationship modelling, transaction processing and Big Data.

resources.name: Physics and Maths Tutor - CS Notes

subjects.name: French
subjects.description: French language, literature, film and cultural studies.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level French (7652) develops advanced linguistic competence across listening, reading, writing and speaking. Themes include social issues and trends, artistic culture, multiculturalism and political life in French-speaking society. Students study one literary text and one film, and complete an individual research project for the speaking exam. Translation tasks require translation into and out of French.

topics.title: Grammar
topics.summary: Tenses, subjunctive mood, pronoun agreement, relative clauses, passive voice, negation and complex sentence structures.

topics.title: Listening
topics.summary: Comprehension of authentic spoken French from news, interviews, lectures and everyday conversations.

topics.title: Speaking
topics.summary: Discussion, debate, stimulus-based conversation and an individual research project presentation.

topics.title: Reading
topics.summary: Literary texts, press articles, inferential comprehension and critical analysis of French-language sources.

topics.title: Writing
topics.summary: Essays on set texts and film, translation into French, summary writing and argumentative composition.

topics.title: Culture
topics.summary: Francophone society, film, literature, music, politics and the influence of French cultural heritage globally.

subjects.name: Further Mathematics
subjects.description: Complex numbers, matrices, differential equations, polar coordinates and hyperbolic functions.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Further Mathematics (7367) builds on A-Level Maths with compulsory core pure content covering complex numbers, matrices, proof, further algebra, further calculus, differential equations, polar coordinates and hyperbolic functions. Students then choose two optional applications from mechanics, statistics, discrete mathematics or additional pure. Assessment is through four 2-hour papers, two of which are core pure and two are the chosen options.

topics.title: Complex Numbers
topics.summary: Argand diagrams, modulus-argument form, de Moivre's theorem, roots of unity and loci in the complex plane.

topics.title: Matrices
topics.summary: Matrix algebra, determinants, inverse matrices, linear transformations, eigenvalues, eigenvectors and diagonalisation.

topics.title: Differential Equations
topics.summary: First and second order ODEs, integrating factors, complementary functions, particular integrals and coupled systems.

topics.title: Polar Coordinates
topics.summary: Sketching polar curves, area enclosed by polar curves and converting between Cartesian and polar forms.

topics.title: Hyperbolic Functions
topics.summary: Definitions, identities, differentiation and integration, inverse hyperbolic functions and solving equations.

topics.title: Further Mechanics
topics.summary: Momentum, impulse, work, energy, power, circular motion, centres of mass and further dynamics.

topics.title: Further Statistics
topics.summary: Discrete and continuous distributions, chi-squared tests, bivariate data, correlation, regression and hypothesis testing.

topics.title: Discrete Mathematics
topics.summary: Graph theory, networks, algorithms, critical path analysis, linear programming and game theory.

resources.name: Physics and Maths Tutor - Further Maths

subjects.name: Psychology
subjects.description: Cognitive, developmental, social, biological, psychopathology and research methods.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Psychology (7182) covers introductory topics (social influence, memory, attachment, psychopathology), psychology in context (approaches, biopsychology, research methods) and issues and options (issues and debates, plus three option topics from relationships, gender, cognition, schizophrenia, eating behaviour, stress, aggression, forensic psychology and addiction). Students develop scientific literacy, research methodology and extended writing skills.

topics.title: Cognitive Psychology
topics.summary: Models of memory, forgetting, eyewitness testimony, cognitive interview and reconstructive memory.

topics.title: Developmental Psychology
topics.summary: Attachment theory, animal studies, deprivation, privation and the influence of early experience on later relationships.

topics.title: Social Psychology
topics.summary: Conformity, obedience, resistance to social influence, minority influence and the role of social change.

topics.title: Biological Psychology
topics.summary: The nervous system, neurons, synaptic transmission, endocrine system, localisation of function and biological rhythms.

topics.title: Psychopathology
topics.summary: Definitions of abnormality, phobias, depression, OCD and explanations from behavioural, cognitive and biological perspectives.

topics.title: Research Methods
topics.summary: Experimental design, sampling, data handling, statistical testing, ethics and practical research procedures.

topics.title: Issues and Debates
topics.summary: Free will vs determinism, nature vs nurture, holism vs reductionism, idiographic vs nomothetic, ethical implications and controversies.

topics.title: Applied Psychology
topics.summary: Optional topics such as schizophrenia, forensic psychology, addiction, relationships, stress and eating behaviour.

subjects.name: Sociology
subjects.description: Education, families, crime, theory, research methods and media.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Sociology (7192) explores the structure of society through education, families and households, beliefs in society, crime and deviance, theory and methods. Students develop a critical understanding of sociological perspectives (functionalism, Marxism, feminism, interactionism, postmodernism) and apply them to contemporary social issues. Assessment is through three 2-hour papers.

topics.title: Education
topics.summary: The role of education, differential achievement by class, gender and ethnicity, education policy and the relationship between schooling and capitalism.

topics.title: Families and Households
topics.summary: Changing family structures, gender roles, childhood, demographic trends, divorce and family diversity in contemporary Britain.

topics.title: Crime and Deviance
topics.summary: Theories of crime, social distribution of crime, globalisation, green crime, state crime, surveillance and crime prevention.

topics.title: Sociological Theory
topics.summary: Functionalism, Marxism, feminism, symbolic interactionism, postmodernism and the debate over sociology as a science.

topics.title: Research Methods
topics.summary: Quantitative and qualitative methods, questionnaires, interviews, observations, experiments, secondary data and ethical considerations.

topics.title: Media
topics.summary: The new media, globalisation and popular culture, media representations of age, gender, ethnicity and class, and the process of selection and presentation of news.

subjects.name: Business Studies
subjects.description: Finance, marketing, operations, HR, strategy and the external environment.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Business (7132) examines business decision-making across marketing, finance, operations and human resources. Students analyse real-world case studies, evaluate strategic choices, and understand the external environment including economic, legal, social and technological influences. A new specification (7138) for first teaching 2025 refines the structure into three focuses: What is Business, Managing Marketing and Finance; Managing Operations and People; and Business Strategy and the External Environment.

topics.title: Marketing
topics.summary: Market research, segmentation, targeting, positioning, the marketing mix, digital marketing, branding and international marketing.

topics.title: Finance
topics.summary: Sources of finance, break-even analysis, cash flow, liquidity, profitability ratios, budgets, financial reporting and investment appraisal.

topics.title: Operations Management
topics.summary: Efficiency, quality management, lean production, supply chain, inventory management, capacity utilisation and technology.

topics.title: Human Resources
topics.summary: Organisational design, leadership, motivation theories, recruitment, training, employee wellbeing and managing business culture.

topics.title: Strategy
topics.summary: SWOT analysis, Ansoff matrix, Porter's generic strategies, growth, globalisation, retrenchment, digital strategy and competitive positioning.

topics.title: External Environment
topics.summary: PESTLE analysis, corporate social responsibility, ESG, legal, economic, social, technological and competitive influences on business.

subjects.name: Art & Design
subjects.description: Fine art, graphic communication, photography, textiles and 3D design.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Art and Design (7201-7206) offers specialisms including Fine Art, Graphic Communication, Photography, Textile Design, Three-Dimensional Design and Art, Craft and Design. Students develop practical and critical skills through a personal investigation (60%) supported by a written study of 1,000–3,000 words, and an externally set assignment (15 hours, 40%). The course emphasises independent thinking, experimentation and the development of a personal visual language.

topics.title: Fine Art
topics.summary: Drawing, painting, mixed media, sculpture, printmaking, moving image and photography as forms of personal expression.

topics.title: Graphic Communication
topics.summary: Advertising, packaging, illustration, branding, interactive media, motion graphics and design for print and screen.

topics.title: Photography
topics.summary: Portraiture, landscape, documentary, fashion, experimental imagery, digital and analogue processes, and photographic installation.

topics.title: Textile Design
topics.summary: Fashion design, printed and dyed fabrics, constructed textiles, art textiles, digital textiles and interior design applications.

topics.title: 3D Design
topics.summary: Sculpture, ceramics, product design, architectural design, jewellery, exhibition design and environmental design.

topics.title: Critical and Contextual Studies
topics.summary: Research into artists, designers and movements, analysis of visual culture, and the relationship between theory and practice.

resources.name: AQA A-Level Art and Design Specification

subjects.name: Politics
subjects.description: UK politics, political ideas, global politics and comparative politics.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Politics (7152) covers the government and politics of the UK, the government and politics of the USA with comparative analysis, and political ideas including liberalism, conservatism, socialism and an optional ideology (feminism, nationalism, multiculturalism, ecologism or anarchism). Students develop skills in analysing political information, constructing arguments and evaluating political institutions and behaviour.

topics.title: UK Politics
topics.summary: Democracy and participation, political parties, electoral systems, voting behaviour, the media and pressure groups.

topics.title: UK Government
topics.summary: The constitution, Parliament, the Prime Minister and Cabinet, the judiciary, and relations between branches of government.

topics.title: US Politics
topics.summary: The US Constitution, Congress, the Presidency, the Supreme Court, federalism, civil rights and the electoral process.

topics.title: Comparative Politics
topics.summary: Comparing UK and US constitutional arrangements, executives, judiciaries, party systems, civil rights and pressure groups.

topics.title: Political Ideas
topics.summary: Liberalism, conservatism, socialism, and one optional ideology such as feminism, nationalism, multiculturalism, ecologism or anarchism.

topics.title: Global Politics
topics.summary: Globalisation, global governance, international organisations, human rights, environmental politics and theories of international relations.

subjects.name: Law
subjects.description: English legal system, criminal law, contract law and tort law.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Law (7162) provides a comprehensive introduction to the English legal system, criminal law, tort law and either contract law or human rights. Students develop analytical and critical thinking skills, learn to apply legal rules to scenarios, and evaluate legal concepts and issues. Assessment is through three 2-hour papers combining multiple choice, short answer and extended writing.

topics.title: English Legal System
topics.summary: Civil and criminal courts, the judiciary, juries, magistrates, legal profession, sources of law and access to justice.

topics.title: Criminal Law
topics.summary: Actus reus, mens rea, offences against the person (murder, manslaughter, assault), theft, robbery and defences.

topics.title: Tort Law
topics.summary: Negligence, duty of care, breach, causation, occupiers' liability, nuisance, vicarious liability and remedies.

topics.title: Contract Law
topics.summary: Formation, terms, vitiating factors, misrepresentation, duress, frustration, breach and remedies for breach of contract.

topics.title: Human Rights
topics.summary: The European Convention on Human Rights, the Human Rights Act 1998, enforcement and restrictions on rights.

topics.title: Nature of Law
topics.summary: Law and morality, law and justice, the rule of law, fault, balancing conflicting interests and the judiciary's role.

subjects.name: Music
subjects.description: Performance, composition, analysis, listening and historical study.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Music (7272) develops performing, composing and appraising skills. Students study works from the Western classical tradition, popular music and jazz, and music for media and theatre. The course includes solo and ensemble performance, free and brief-based composition, and analysis of set works through listening and written examination. Assessment includes coursework (40%) and a 2.5-hour written exam (40%).

topics.title: Performance
topics.summary: Solo and ensemble recital performance, interpretation, technical control, stylistic awareness and programme notes.

topics.title: Composition
topics.summary: Free composition and composition to a brief, harmonic language, texture, structure, orchestration and notation.

topics.title: Listening and Analysis
topics.summary: Aural perception, dictation, harmonic analysis, score reading and identification of musical features.

topics.title: Western Classical Tradition
topics.summary: Set works from Baroque, Classical, Romantic and twentieth-century orchestral and choral repertoire.

topics.title: Popular Music and Jazz
topics.summary: Study of popular music styles, jazz, blues, rock, pop and their social and cultural contexts.

topics.title: Music for Media and Theatre
topics.summary: Film music, musical theatre, programme music and the relationship between music and dramatic narrative.

subjects.name: Drama & Theatre
subjects.description: Devising, text performance, theatre makers and critical analysis.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Drama and Theatre (7262) combines practical theatre-making with academic study. Students devise original work informed by a practitioner, perform extracts from published plays applying a different practitioner's methodology, and analyse live theatre and set texts in a written exam. Assessment includes practical performance (60%) and written examination (40%).

topics.title: Devising Theatre
topics.summary: Creating original performance from a stimulus, documenting the process in a working notebook and applying practitioner methodologies.

topics.title: Text Performance
topics.summary: Performing key extracts from three different plays, interpreting character and applying theatrical techniques.

topics.title: Theatre Makers
topics.summary: Studying the work and methodologies of influential practitioners such as Stanislavski, Brecht, Artaud, Boal and Brook.

topics.title: Live Theatre Analysis
topics.summary: Evaluating acting, directing, design and the creative intentions of professional productions as an audience member.

topics.title: Set Text Study
topics.summary: In-depth analysis of two set plays, exploring interpretation, staging, context and the relationship between text and performance.

topics.title: Theatre Design
topics.summary: Lighting, sound, set and costume design, and how design elements contribute to meaning and theatrical effect.

resources.name: AQA A-Level Drama and Theatre Specification (7262)
OCR A-Level Drama and Theatre Specification

subjects.name: Physical Education
subjects.description: Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, psychology and sport in society.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Physical Education (7582) combines scientific theory with practical performance. Students study applied anatomy and physiology, exercise physiology, biomechanics, skill acquisition, sport psychology, and sport in society and technology. Practical assessment requires performance or coaching in one approved activity, plus a written/verbal analysis of performance. Assessment is two 2-hour exams (35% each) and practical NEA (30%).

topics.title: Applied Anatomy and Physiology
topics.summary: Cardiovascular, respiratory, musculoskeletal and energy systems, and their adaptation to exercise.

topics.title: Exercise Physiology
topics.summary: Training methods, fatigue, recovery, injury prevention, and the physiological effects of altitude and heat.

topics.title: Biomechanics
topics.summary: Newton's laws, linear and angular motion, projectile motion, levers, stability and analysis of sporting movements.

topics.title: Skill Acquisition
topics.summary: Theories of learning, stages of learning, feedback, guidance, practice structures and information processing models.

topics.title: Sport Psychology
topics.summary: Personality, motivation, arousal, anxiety, aggression, group dynamics, leadership and goal setting.

topics.title: Sport and Society
topics.summary: Commercialisation, media, ethics, deviance, discrimination, the role of technology and the development of sport.

subjects.name: Spanish
subjects.description: Spanish language, literature, film and cultural studies.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Spanish (7692) develops advanced linguistic competence across listening, reading, writing and speaking. Themes include social issues and trends, artistic culture, multiculturalism and political life in Hispanic society. Students study one literary text and one film, and complete an individual research project for the speaking exam. Translation tasks require translation into and out of Spanish.

topics.title: Grammar
topics.summary: Advanced tenses, subjunctive mood, pronoun agreement, relative clauses, passive voice, negation and complex sentence structures.

topics.title: Listening
topics.summary: Comprehension of authentic spoken Spanish from news, interviews, lectures and everyday conversations in Hispanic contexts.

topics.title: Speaking
topics.summary: Discussion, debate, stimulus-based conversation and an individual research project presentation on a chosen topic.

topics.title: Reading
topics.summary: Literary texts, press articles, inferential comprehension and critical analysis of Spanish-language sources.

topics.title: Writing
topics.summary: Essays on set texts and film, translation into Spanish, summary writing and argumentative composition.

topics.title: Hispanic Culture
topics.summary: Spanish and Latin American society, film, literature, music, politics, regional identity and cultural heritage.

subjects.name: German
subjects.description: German language, literature, film and cultural studies.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level German (7662) develops advanced linguistic competence across listening, reading, writing and speaking. Themes include social issues and trends, artistic culture, multiculturalism and political life in German-speaking society. Students study one literary text and one film, and complete an individual research project for the speaking exam. Translation tasks require translation into and out of German.

topics.title: Grammar
topics.summary: Advanced tenses, subjunctive mood, case system, adjective endings, word order, passive voice and complex sentence structures.

topics.title: Listening
topics.summary: Comprehension of authentic spoken German from news, interviews, lectures and everyday conversations in German-speaking contexts.

topics.title: Speaking
topics.summary: Discussion, debate, stimulus-based conversation and an individual research project presentation on a chosen topic.

topics.title: Reading
topics.summary: Literary texts, press articles, inferential comprehension and critical analysis of German-language sources.

topics.title: Writing
topics.summary: Essays on set texts and film, translation into German, summary writing and argumentative composition.

topics.title: German-speaking Culture
topics.summary: German and Austrian society, film, literature, music, politics, reunification, immigration and cultural heritage.

subjects.name: Religious Studies
subjects.description: Philosophy, ethics, developments in religious thought and Christian theology.
subjects.summary: AQA A-Level Religious Studies (7062) combines Philosophy of Religion, Ethics and the Study of a Religion (typically Christianity). Students examine arguments for the existence of God, the problem of evil, religious language, ethical theories (natural law, virtue ethics, utilitarianism, situation ethics), and developments in Christian thought including gender, liberation theology, and the relationship between religion and science. Assessment is through two 3-hour papers.

topics.title: Philosophy of Religion
topics.summary: Arguments for God's existence, the problem of evil, religious experience, religious language, the nature of God and miracles.

topics.title: Ethics
topics.summary: Natural moral law, virtue ethics, Kantian ethics, utilitarianism, situation ethics, euthanasia, business ethics and sexual ethics.

topics.title: Developments in Christian Thought
topics.summary: Augustine's teachings, human nature, death and the afterlife, knowledge of God, the person of Jesus, Christian moral principles and action.

topics.title: Religious Language
topics.summary: Verification and falsification, analogy, symbol, myth, the via negativa and Wittgenstein's language games.

topics.title: Christianity and Society
topics.summary: Gender and society, gender and theology, secularism, liberation theology, Marxism and Christian responses to pluralism.

topics.title: Religion and Science
